original_hypothesis.title: Asset lock / denial of execution if owner renounces ownership before setting critical addresses (manager/accountant/feeWallet rotation impossible)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: HypoVault relies on the owner to rotate manager/accountant and set feeWallet. If ownership is renounced (or transferred to an unusable address), the protocol can become operationally unrecoverable: manager cannot be rotated if compromised/unresponsive, accountant cannot be replaced if it misprices NAV, and feeWallet cannot be set to avoid executeWithdrawal reverting on profitable withdrawals (token-dependent). This can freeze or brick core flows long-term.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an operational dead-end state risk stemming from reliance on Ownable governance. While it requires privileged action/misconfiguration, it is a common real-world failure mode that permanently impacts user funds.
deployment_assumptions: Owner renounces ownership or transfers ownership incorrectly
feeWallet remains unset or is set to an address that reverts on receiving tokens
Manager/accountant later require rotation to restore operations
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment scripts provided; cannot confirm whether ownership is transferred/renounced or feeWallet is set post-deploy.

proof_of_signal.pattern: OnlyOwner setters for manager/accountant/feeWallet; ownership controls recovery but can be renounced via inherited Ownable

preconditions: Owner privileges are lost (renounceOwnership or transfer to wrong address)
A later incident requires owner action (rotate manager/accountant, set feeWallet)
Users depend on those actions to execute withdrawals or maintain NAV correctness

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Owner is the only recovery lever; no secondary governance or safety valve in code

operational_questions: What is the intended ownership lifecycle (EOA, multisig, timelock, renounce)?
Is there a runbook preventing renounceOwnership before initialization steps?
Are there monitoring controls that block accidental renounce in operations?
validation_requirements: Review deployment process/runbooks (if any) for ownership transfer and feeWallet initialization
Simulate incident recovery: manager compromise + no owner ability to rotate

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Wait for ownership to be renounced/mis-set (operational mistake)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Exploit ensuing inability to rotate manager/accountant/feeWallet to force prolonged DoS or mispricing conditions
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Profit indirectly (e.g., secondary market mispricing of shares) if applicable
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Requires privileged misconfiguration; attacker may simply capitalize after the fact

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Potential permanent inability to fix bricked epochs or mispriced NAV inputs
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: manager rotation, accountant rotation, feeWallet updates

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Ownership set to address(0) or an address without known control
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Long periods without fulfillments; inability to respond to incidents

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
